title: I built a trading journal with AI coaching, confluence tracking, and performance heatmaps — here's the demo
author: Local-Amphibian9197
selftext:
Most traders know they should journal. Few actually do it consistently — and even fewer review their data in a way that leads to real improvement.

That's the problem I tried to solve with [TradingSFX](http://www.tradingsfx.com).

It's a web-based trading journal built for forex, indices, and crypto traders. You log your trades with entry, exit, strategy, confluences, and chart screenshots. From there, the platform does the heavy lifting:

— **Confluence analysis** that shows which conditions actually predict winning setups

— **Equity curve tracking** with peak, trough, and max drawdown 

— **Strategy breakdown** so you can see breakeven win rate per setup (ICT, SMC, S&D, whatever you use) 

— **Performance heatmap** that reveals your best day/hour windows 

— **AI coaching** that identifies patterns in your trading — what works, when you overtrade, where your edge really is

There's a free plan with no credit card required. You can try the full demo here and see everything in action:

👉 [https://tradingsfx.com/demo](https://tradingsfx.com/demo)

Would love to hear feedback from anyone who journals already — what's missing from the tools you've tried?

comments.author: bjxxjj
comments.body:
Looks clean from the demo. I like the focus on confluence tracking — most journals just log P/L and call it a day, but edge usually comes from combinations, not single signals.

A couple questions:

1) How customizable are the confluence tags? Can users define their own rule sets and later filter performance by *specific combinations* (e.g., HTF bias + liquidity sweep + NY session)?
2) Does the AI coaching give actionable stats-based feedback (e.g., “you underperform in low-volatility sessions”) or is it more generic behavioral advice?
3) How do you handle screenshot organization and review flow? For me, fast replay/review is more important than logging.

Also curious how this compares to TraderVue/Edgewonk in terms of depth vs simplicity.  

Solid concept overall — consistency + structured review is the real edge.
